cards.back: le médecin qui soigne des patients
cards.grammarNote: qui = who/that (subject of the relative clause). The verb following qui agrees with the antecedent: le médecin qui soigne (3rd person singular).
que = whom/that (direct object of the relative clause). I is the subject; the work is the object. que cannot be omitted in French (unlike 'that' in English).
qui is the subject (the colleague did the helping). In passé composé: qui m'a aidé. The participle agrees with qui's antecedent only if it's feminine.
que (object pronoun). I = subject, the job = object. Tip: if the relative clause already has both a subject and a verb, use que (the noun is the missing object).
qui is the subject of its clause. qui replaces a person or thing as the subject: le client (he) a appelé → le client qui a appelé.

cards.back: le projet que nous avons terminé
cards.grammarNote: que is the object. With avoir in passé composé, the past participle agrees with a preceding direct object: que (= le projet, masc.) → terminé (no change here).

que elides to qu' before a vowel: que j'adore → qu'j'adore? No — it stays que j'adore (the j in j'adore begins the subject pronoun). Actually: que + j' → que j'adore. The elision is on je → j', not on que.

cards.back: la réunion à laquelle j'ai assisté
cards.grammarNote: assister à quelque chose → when à is involved, use à + lequel/laquelle/lesquels/lesquelles instead of que. This is an advanced form — qui/que only work when there is no preposition.
que elides to qu' before a vowel: que j'admire → qu'? The subject here is j', so elision happens on je, not que: le collègue que j'admire.

cards.front: the office that is open on Saturdays
cards.back: le bureau qui est ouvert le samedi
cards.grammarNote: qui can refer to things as well as people: le bureau qui est ouvert (the office that is open). qui = subject of 'est ouvert'.
que works with plural antecedents too. les tâches que j'aime = the tasks (that) I love. In passé composé the participle would agree: les tâches que j'ai aimées.
Quick test: if you can say 'he/she [verb]' (the boss listens → he listens), use qui. If you can say '[subject] [verb] him/her/it' (I do it → use que).
